entry.title: Auth0 Flask Tools
entry.summary: Another Auth0 project, this time using Flask and the Auth0 python SDK. First up: build my own interface for managing users in an Auth0 hosted database.
entry.content: Another Auth0 project, this time using Flask and the Auth0 python SDK. First up: build my own interface for managing users in an Auth0 hosted database.

entry.title: Auth0 Command Line Utilities
entry.summary: Not to be confused with my authzero command line interface, this repo contains general purpose python scripts for interfacing with the Auth0 Management API.
entry.content: Not to be confused with my authzero command line interface, this repo contains general purpose python scripts for interfacing with the Auth0 Management API.

entry.title: authzero - A CLI for the Auth0 Management API
entry.summary:
I started work on a command line interface for the Auth0 Management API. The tool is built with TypeScript, oclif, node.js, and Auth0’s node-auth0 SDK.

$ authzero --help
Auth0 Command Line Interface

VERSION
  authzero/0.0.0 darwin-x64 node-v11.6.0

USAGE
  $ authzero [COMMAND]

COMMANDS
  client      Get client (application …
entry.content:
I started work on a command line interface for the Auth0 Management API. The tool is built with TypeScript, oclif, node.js, and Auth0’s node-auth0 SDK.

$ authzero --help
Auth0 Command Line Interface

VERSION
  authzero/0.0.0 darwin-x64 node-v11.6.0

USAGE
  $ authzero [COMMAND]

COMMANDS
  client      Get client (application) settings.
  connection  Get connection settings.
  help        display help for authzero
  rule        Get rule.
  tenant      Get tenant settings.
  user        Get profile for specificed user.

$

entry.title: My First Pull Request
entry.summary:
I use Pelican and the pelican-bootstrap3 theme to built this site.

I made my first pull request today in the form of a minor contribution to pelican-bootstrap3. I added a feature that allows you to add a series of images to the sidebar. The original intent was to allow me …
entry.content:
I use Pelican and the pelican-bootstrap3 theme to built this site.

I made my first pull request today in the form of a minor contribution to pelican-bootstrap3. I added a feature that allows you to add a series of images to the sidebar. The original intent was to allow me to add certification marks to my sidebar, which you can now see in action right here!

Props to @DandyDev for the pro tips re: submitting good PRs.
